column1: // Golden cases for kparser. Format: <input><TAB><expected AST>.
// Lines beginning with // are comments; blank lines are ignored.
// Expected value @ABORT means the input must make the parser exit nonzero.
// Grouped so that every route through the scanner, parser, and printer is hit.
// ---- the three nothings: () is the empty list, an elided slot is the
//      implicit null (prints as nothing), :: is the written identity ----
// ---- projections: an elided argument slot is the implicit null; it prints
//      as nothing, unlike an explicit :: (same value, different spelling) ----
// ---- parse_term: adverbs  v : tA  (all six adverbs) ----
// ---- parse_e: nve with primitive verbs (every verb in the table) ----
// ---- parse_e: monadic demotion (KV2 -> KV1) and explicit monadic tokens ----
// ---- parse_e: derived verb in infix position (the nve/tA fix) ----
// ---- compositions: te juxtaposition of two functions ----
// ---- parse_E: top-level semicolons are a statement SEQUENCE (`;), not a
//      list -- contrast (1;2;3) above, which is the list literal ----
// ---- scanner: sign vs subtract (the noun_pos rule) ----
// ---- scanner: '_' name vs drop/floor (the name_start rule) ----
// At token start, '_' begins a name iff a name char follows; otherwise it
// is the verb. Inside a name it always continues. Purely lexical, so _abc
// is a name everywhere -- but 1_abc juxtaposes, use whitespace (1_ abc)
// ---- abort routes (die): unknown characters are rejected, not skipped ----
